channel.title: Career on VENANT 🐦
channel.description: Recent content in Career on VENANT 🐦

item.title: The Death of Conventional IT Workers: The Mahoraga Adaptation
item.description:
Entering the IT industry no longer guarantees a comfort zone. Low-level IT jobs—boilerplate coders, basic sysadmins, L1/L2 support, and manual QA—are heavily commoditized by AI. The value of an IT professional has shifted from typing speed to the ability to design systems, secure architecture, and orchestrate AI agents.

Tech companies aren’t executing mass layoffs because they’re going bankrupt. They are reallocating capital. Headcounts are slashed to buy GPUs and build AI infrastructure.

item.title: Beyond the 12-Year Trap: Becoming the Architect of Intent
item.description:
The anxiety is real. You spend twelve years in a classroom. You memorize dates, formulas, and procedures. You qualify as a specialist. Then, a few lines of code appear that can do your week’s work in ten seconds. It feels like a betrayal.

But this is not the end of work. It is the end of drudgery.

For decades, our education system treated humans as biological processors. We were trained to be reliable, predictable, and fast at execution. These are exactly the traits that machines dominate. If your value is based on following a manual, you are competing with a tool that never sleeps and has read every manual ever written.
